items.headline: Mountain View updates Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a draft update to the Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan during a study session on April 28. This plan guides how the city manages trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat—affecting everything from street tree planting to park conservation.

items.headline: Foothills Nature Preserve improvements project discussed
items.summary: The City Council will receive an informational update on the Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project (PE-21000), detailing planned enhancements to this public green space.

items.headline: City considers future model for middle school athletics
items.summary: The Council will discuss management options and program direction for Palo Alto's middle school athletics program, which currently faces decisions about how it should be run and funded.

items.headline: City releases planning activities and economic strategy annual reports
items.summary: The council received semi-annual updates on citywide planning activities, major events, and the economic strategy work plan. These reports provide insight into the city's development priorities and initiatives affecting South Bay residents over the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans 67-unit mixed-use project with retail and housing
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on a proposed 4.22-acre development that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartment units alongside 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story retail building while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project requires subdivision into 17 lots and would represent significant residential and commercial growth in that area.

items.headline: Protected tree removal disputed at 751 Waverley Street
items.summary: A property owner is challenging a previously approved permit to remove a protected tree at 751 Waverley Street. The city is reviewing whether the original approval should stand.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved changes to business license tax rates under an ordinance amending the Campbell Municipal Code, effective in the new fiscal year. This annual update may affect local business costs and city revenue.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City Council withdraws supergraphic signs ordinance
items.summary: Santa Clara was set to vote on allowing large promotional signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events, but the city staff withdrew the item before a decision. The decision means the signage rules for the World Cup remain unchanged.
